details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands (get pods -A, get events -A, logs, describe, get endpoints, get nodes --show-labels) and correctly identified all 14 broken namespaces with plausible, evidence-backed root causes: fault-crashloop (DB connection refused, confirmed by logs), fault-oom (OOMKilled exit 137, confirmed by describe), fault-imagepull (nonexistent image tag, confirmed by describe), fault-readiness (probe port 8080 vs container port 80, confirmed by spec inspection), fault-configmap (missing ConfigMap, confirmed by events), fault-init (migration lock, confirmed by logs), fault-unschedulable (node selector mismatch, confirmed by node labels), fault-pvc (StorageClass not found, confirmed by events), fault-endpoints (service selector mismatch app:web-backend-v2 vs pods labeled app:web-backend, confirmed by svc yaml), fault-rollout (bad image tag in new RS, confirmed by events), fault-multicontainer (DNS failure in log-shipper, confirmed by logs), shop-checkout (missing secret + web crashloop with DB DNS failure), payments (unschedulable node selector), and inventory (quota blocks 2/3 replicas, confirmed by FailedCreate events). The healthy and quiet-ops namespaces were correctly not flagged. The agent identified 14/14 broken namespaces, well above the 12/14 threshold, with all diagnoses backed by actual command output.
